heroHeadline: Berlin Conference Boycotts Escalate; 40,000+ Flee El Fasher Amid Ongoing RSF Operations
heroSubtitle: Multiple Sudanese political factions including the National Forces Alliance and National Forces Coordination have formally boycotted the Third Berlin Conference on Sudan (scheduled April 15), citing exclusion and representation disputes; over 40,000 people (~8,100 households) have been newly displaced from the El Fasher area in North Darfur as RSF military pressure continues following its October 2025 capture — compounding a humanitarian emergency the UN Fact-Finding Mission characterized as bearing 'hallmarks of genocide'; Lt. Gen. Yasser al-Atta took formal command as SAF Chief of Staff in a handover ceremony witnessed by Gen. al-Burhan on April 6, completing the largest SAF command shakeup since the war began; Sudan's 2026 humanitarian appeal remains 84% underfunded at $2.9B requested
footerNote: Data sourced from UN OCHA, UNHCR, WFP, OHCHR, WHO, ACLED, HRW, Radio Dabanga, Sudan Tribune, Reuters, Al Jazeera, MSF, Darfur24, AFP, Anadolu Agency, The National, UN News, UN Security Council, IOM, Washington Post, MadaMasr, Xinhua, Asharq Al-Awsat, Sudan Horizon
